results.title: Batik — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: Pure source-domain Wikipedia article on batik craft. Snippet mentions 'wax-resist dyeing', 'canting', 'dye iterations' but none of the multi-word content words appear as exact substrings: 'batik wax resist' is split across title+snippet (title has 'Batik' alone, snippet has 'wax-resist' with hyphen), 'canting wax application' has only 'canting' as substring, 'dye-stage schedule' has 'dye iterations' not the candidate phrase. No LLM/fine-tune mechanism.

results.title: Batik — Britannica
results.judge_reason: Source-domain Britannica entry on batik process; snippet describes 'stages of wax + dye + boil-off process'. None of the multi-word content_words appear as exact substrings. Pure cultural/craft content; no LLM, no mask stack, no fine-tuning mechanism.

results.title: Batik Tulis Indonesia — iBatik
results.judge_reason: Source-domain page on Indonesian batik tulis using the canting pen. 'canting' appears as a single word in snippet but the full multi-word content word 'canting wax application' is not a substring. No LLM/fine-tune mechanism.

results.title: Defying Catastrophic Forgetting in Continual LLM Unlearning (2026/1)
results.judge_reason: FIT continual unlearning with importance-aware selection + targeted layer attribution operates across a stream of unlearning operations and selectively focuses parameter changes, which loosely resembles selective protection across sequential stages. However, FIT is an UNLEARNING framework (removing capabilities) not a fine-tune-while-protecting wax stack, and there is no explicit MONOTONICALLY-GROWING union mask across N stages nor a coordinate-to-task offline assignment computed from a wax schedule. Functional equivalence below 0.7 threshold. None of the multi-word content words match as exact substrings.

results.title: Mechanistic Analysis of Catastrophic Forgetting (2026/1)
results.judge_reason: Mechanistic analysis of why forgetting happens (attention disruption + representational drift + loss-landscape flattening); purely diagnostic, not a mitigation/protection mechanism. Does not propose any wax-style coordinate protection, mask stack, or sequential dye protocol. No content words match as substrings.

results.title: Mitigating Forgetting via Low-Perplexity Token Learning (NeurIPS 2025)
results.judge_reason: Applies a token-mask (on high-perplexity tokens) to suppress gradient updates and preserve non-target task performance — operationally a per-stage selective gradient mask in single fine-tune. However, (a) the mask is on tokens, not on parameter coordinates as the candidate specifies; (b) it is single-stage, with no monotonically-growing union mask across N sequential dye-stages; (c) there is no coordinate-to-task offline assignment computed from a wax stack. Functional equivalence below 0.7 threshold. No content words match as substrings.

results.title: Hierarchical Layer-Wise & Element-Wise Regularization (2025/1)
results.judge_reason: Element-wise + layer-wise importance regularization for continual fine-tune is an EWC-style soft per-coordinate protection that, when applied across sequential stages, accumulates protection on previously-important coordinates — loosely a soft wax stack. However, the paper does not explicitly construct a discrete monotonically-growing union mask across N stages, does not compute a coordinate-to-task-list assignment offline from a 'wax schedule', and uses continuous regularization weights instead of a stack-of-masks discipline. Functional equivalence at 0.6, below the 0.7 threshold. No multi-word content words match as exact substrings.

results.title: Mitigating Catastrophic Forgetting via Model Growth (2025/9)
results.judge_reason: Model-growth approach adds new capacity for new tasks rather than protecting (waxing) prior-task coordinates within fixed parameters. Operationally orthogonal to the candidate's stack-of-masks-over-fixed-parameters discipline; no coordinate-to-task assignment from a wax schedule. No content words match as substrings.

fail_reason: No result satisfied any branch of the OR rule. Max functional_equivalence was 0.6 (rank 6, Hierarchical Layer-Wise & Element-Wise Regularization — soft per-coordinate protection across continual fine-tune, but missing the explicit monotonically-growing union mask stack across N sequential dye-stages and the offline coordinate-to-task assignment computed from a wax schedule). Max semantic_cosine_estimate was 0.42. Max keyword_overlap_count was 0 across all 8 results — none of the multi-word content words ('batik wax resist', 'canting wax application', 'dye-stage schedule', 'wax mask stack', 'monotonically growing protection', 'coordinate-to-task assignment', 'sequential dye protocol', 'stack-from-schedule deployed model') appear as exact substrings in any title+snippet. The candidate's batik wax-stack fine-tuning discipline is not represented as a hit in any of the 8 results.
